revision.text:
[[en:Futaji Irihime]][[ja:両道入姫命]][[zh:兩道入姬命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|spouse={{ill|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}|children={{ill|Chūai|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}; {{ill|Q106382191|qid=Q106382191|1=Inayoribetsu no Ō}}|father={{ill|Suinin|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|mother={{ill|Q109931034|qid=Q109931034|1=Kihobe}}}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Futaji Iribime no Mikoto | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | relationship = {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}’s daughter
Mother of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} | titles = | full_name = | Rank = Grand Empress Dowager | honorific_prefix = | seal = | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = Unknown | death_place = | burial_date = | burial_place = | spouse1 = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} | children = Inaiwake no Ō, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, Nunoshihime no Mikoto, Wakatake no Ō | father = {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} | mother = Kanihatobe (daughter of Ōkuni no Fuchi from Yamashiro Province) | office = | religion = | signature = }} '''Futaji Iribime no Mikoto''' (両道入姫命, birth and death dates unknown) is a figure in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. She was a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, born to Kanihatobe (also referred to in ''Kojiki'' as Oto Karuhatade no Tobeno), the daughter of Ōkuni no Fuchi from Yamashiro Province. In ''Nihon Shoki,'' she is called '''Futaji Iribime no Mikoto''', while ''Kojiki'' refers to her as '''Ihatsukubime''' (石衝毘売命). She had a full brother named {{ill|Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto|ja|%E7%A3%90%E8%A1%9D%E5%88%A5%E5%91%BD|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|1=Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto|2=ja|3=磐衝別命|qid=Q11587884}}. == Summary == Futaji Iribime became the consort of her nephew, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, and gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}. She was later named Grand Empress Dowager upon his ascension to the throne. According to ''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Chūai proclaimed on the 1st day of the 9th month of his first year that "his mother, the Empress, would be revered as the Grand Empress Dowager." However, since Yamato Takeru never ascended to the throne, she could not have been an empress during her husband’s lifetime. Both ''Kojiki'' and ''Nihon Shoki'' omit specific details about her life and deeds. Additionally, there is a significant chronological inconsistency: the records state Emperor Chūai was born in the 18th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, which is 38 years after Yamato Takeru's death. This raises doubts about the historical accuracy of Futaji Iribime no Mikoto’s existence and her connection to Yamato Takeru and Emperor Chūai. == Genealogy == {{Generations of Jimmu}} == References == ===Notes=== === References === === Bibliography === * Y. Shimura (Ed.), ''Tenno Kōzoku Rekishi Densetsu Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Imperial History and Legends), Bensei Publishing, 2008. ISBN 978-4-585-06063-5 (Entry on "Emperor Chūai" by Takaaki Kiryu), p. 59. {{Consorts of Japan}} {{translated page|ja|両道入姫命|version=88003626|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Futajiiri-hime]] [[ja:両道入姫命]] [[zh:兩道入姬命]] {{wikidata link|Q65248127}} [[Category:Consorts of the Japanese imperial family]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]
